page.content: The automotive semiconductor landscape is undergoing significant transformation, driven by advancements in process node migration and the growing adoption of advanced packaging technologies.

lines.text: The automotive semiconductor landscape is undergoing significant transformation, driven by advancements in process node
migration and the growing adoption of advanced packaging technologies.

page.content: As illustrated in the accompanying tables, companies are progressively transitioning from established nodes to more advanced architectures, with the 7nm process node projected to yield a cost reduction in manufacturing as evidenced by 7nm's Cost of Goods Sold at CHF450M.

page.content: Furthermore, investment in research and development remains essential; for instance, R&D Investment has been allocated at CHF180M, demonstrating a commitment to innovation in this sector.

lines.text: Furthermore, investment in research and development remains essential; for instance, R&D Investment has been allocated at
CHF180M, demonstrating a commitment to innovation in this sector.

page.content: Additionally, the automotive chip supply status indicates an increasing demand, with Units Sold reaching 52,000 units in recent analyses. The importance of Europe in this context cannot be understated, as it is expected to capture a significant share of the market, with projections indicating that 25% of total automotive chips will be produced in the region. Notably, advanced packaging adoption is also on the rise, aligning with the industry's shift towards more integrated and efficient semiconductor solutions. Consequently, stakeholders are urged to monitor these metrics closely, including the gross margin percentage, which currently stands at 38%, to effectively navigate this evolving market landscape.
